---
title: "Fundermentals Of R"
subtitle: '5.0 R Operations'
author: "Prepared By Clinton Moshe Mwachia"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Introduction

Operators are symbols or characters used to perform various mathematical, logical, and relational operations on data objects. These operators are used to perform calculations, compare values, combine data, and manipulate variables in R. Understanding the different types of operators in R is crucial for effective data analysis and programming in R. 

In this context, this note provides an overview of some of the most commonly used operators in R. They include:- 

    1. Assignment operators.
    2. Arithmetic operators.
    3. Comparison operators.
    4. Logical operators.
    5. concatenation operators. 
    
## Arithmetic operators

These operators are used to perform mathematical operations such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, exponentiation, and modulus. These operations are illustrated below.

### Addition

```{r}
# adding two numbers
2 + 100
```

### Subtraction

```{r}
# subtracting two numbers
2000 - 100
```

### Multiplication

```{r}
# multiplying two numbers
44 * 90
```

### Division

```{r}
# dividing two numbers
1000 / 20
```

### Exponentiation

```{r}
# exponentiation
2^3
```

### Modulus

```{r}
# modulus
100 %% 2
# remainder?
100 %% 3
```

## Assignment operators

The assignment operator is used to assign values to variables. You can use either the equal sign or the leftward arrow to assign a value to a variable, both scenarios are illustrated below.

### Using An Equal Sign 

```{r}
# using an equal sign
x = 10
```

THE VALUE OF X IS `r x`

### Using A leftward arrow 

```{r}
# using an equal sign
x <- 20
```

THE NEW VALUE OF X IS `r x`

## Comparison operators

These operators are used to compare values. They will always return a Boolean value.

### Equal to

```{r}
10 == 15
```

### Not Equal to

```{r}
20 != 15
```

### Less Than or Equal to

```{r}
2 <= 15
```

### Greater Than or Equal to

```{r}
20 >= 15
```

### Less Than

```{r}
100 < 150
```

### Greater Than

```{r}
100 > 1020
```

## Logical operators

Logical operators are used to perform logical operations.

### Logical AND Operator

```{r}
TRUE & TRUE

TRUE & FALSE
```

### Logical OR Operator

```{r}
TRUE | TRUE

TRUE | FALSE
```

### Logical NOT Operator

```{r}
!TRUE

!(2==5)
```

## concatenation operators

This operator is used to combine variables into a vector.

```{r}
y = c(1,2,3)

y
```

# Assignement

This is the end of the lesson, Proceed to the assignment.

GOOD LUCK!!
